documents.title: Path Derivation Spec
documents.description:
Path derivation trims assembly namespace prefixes so the viewer hierarchy
    stays focused on meaningful folders.

documents.title: Async Rules Spec
documents.description:
Specification-style [Rule] and [RuleOutline] methods support async/await,
    allowing asynchronous assertions and data-driven async tests.

tests.title: Async rule outline
Async rule executes correctly

documents.title: Scenario Statement Spec
documents.description:
The [Scenario] attribute marks a method as a BDD scenario. It supports
    explicit display names (overriding the method name), descriptions,
    and tags for filtering.

documents.title: Rule Outline Complex Types Spec
documents.description:
[RuleOutline] supports complex types such as enums in [Example] data.
    Values are passed as strongly-typed method parameters by xUnit.

tests.title: Weekday detection

documents.title: Paradigm Validation Spec
documents.description:
LiveDoc enforces paradigm rules: [Scenario] must be inside [Feature],
    [Rule] must be inside [Specification], and a class cannot mix both.
    Violations produce actionable error messages with fix suggestions.

Named values in step descriptions use the <name:value> syntax.

    The display title shows only the value (name is removed).

documents.title: Async Execution
documents.description:
Steps in LiveDoc scenarios support async/await. Given, When, and Then
    steps can each be asynchronous, and async works with context access
    and scenario outlines.

tests.title: Scenario: Multiple async steps
Scenario: Async Given step
Async scenario outline

documents.title: Scenario Execution
documents.description:
Scenarios are isolated from each other and do not share state.
    All step types (Given, When, Then, And, But) execute in
    declaration order within a scenario.

tests.title: Scenario: Second scenario
Scenario: First scenario
Scenario: All step types execute in order

documents.title: Rule Outline Spec
documents.description:
RuleOutline is the specification pattern equivalent of ScenarioOutline.
    It allows data-driven testing with [Example] attributes.
    The rule description can include <placeholder> syntax.

LiveDocValue wraps a raw string and provides type-safe conversion

    AsDateTime(), and As<T>() for enums, arrays, and nullable types.

LiveDocValueArray provides bounds-checked indexing over extracted values.

    throwing LiveDocValueIndexException with helpful context on out-of-range access.

documents.title: Method Name Placeholders Spec
documents.description:
Method names can contain _ALLCAPS placeholders that are replaced
    with parameter values at runtime. This provides self-documenting
    test names without requiring explicit description attributes.
    
    Rules:
    - ALLCAPS segments are matched to parameter names (case-insensitive)
    - Underscores are converted to spaces after placeholder replacement
    - Unmatched placeholders remain as-is

tests.title: Square of <n> is <result>
Cube root of <n> is <result>
Converting <input> to uppercase returns <expected>
LEFT is greater than <right>
STRING is empty
Simple method name without placeholders
Dividing <dividend> by <divisor> equals <quotient>
VALUE is zero
Length of <str> is <len>
TOTAL split in half is <result>
FIRST equals <second>
Adding <a> and <b> returns <result>

documents.title: Step Quoted Values
documents.description:
Single-quoted values in step descriptions are automatically extracted
    into ctx.Step.Values. The array supports indexing, tuple deconstruction,
    typed conversion, and bounds-checked access with helpful errors.

tests.title: Scenario: ValuesRaw contains raw strings before coercion

documents.title: Step Result Response Body Spec
documents.description: StepResult.ResponseBody extracts the HTTP response body from httpYac output, skipping status lines, headers, and assertion markers.

tests.title: Returns null when output is empty
Extracts JSON body from typical httpYac output between headers and assertion markers
Returns null when output has no HTTP status line
Stops collecting body at 'N requests processed' summary line
Handles multi-line pretty-printed JSON body
Stops collecting body at '[-]' assertion marker
Returns null when there is a status line but no body between headers and assertions
Strips ANSI escape codes from body lines

documents.title: Tag Attribute Spec
documents.description:
The [Tag] attribute enables categorization and filtering of tests.
    Tags can be applied at class and method level, are merged together,
    and deduplicated in a case-insensitive manner.

tests.title: Comma-separated tags are parsed into individual values
Class and method tags are merged
Class without tags returns empty array
Whitespace around tags is trimmed
Multiple Tag attributes on a class are merged
Duplicate tags are deduplicated
Tags on a class are collected
Single tag is parsed correctly
Tags on a method are collected
Deduplication is case-insensitive
Empty strings produce no tags
